items.title: Westpac IQ: Australia's data centre investment pipeline exceeds A$155 billion
items.summary: Pipeline now equals ~5.6% of one year's GDP. Deployable capacity expected to more than double from ~1,350 MW (2024) to 3,100+ MW by 2030, requiring ~A$26bn of new investment.

items.title: Microsoft commits A$25 billion to Australian AI infrastructure, security and skills by 2029
items.summary: Microsoft's largest-ever Australian commitment: A$25bn (≈US$18bn) in new digital and AI infrastructure by end of 2029, expanding its hyperscale footprint and skilling programs.

items.title: Canberra sets national expectations for data centre and AI infrastructure developers
items.source: Dept of Industry, Science and Resources
items.summary: New national expectations covering energy, water, community benefit and sovereignty form the 'social licence to operate' for developers — the rulebook for every build-out on this feed.

items.title: Investment Delivery Authority fast-tracks 15 data centre projects worth A$51.9 billion
items.summary: The IDA endorsed 15 data centre projects worth A$51.9bn for prioritised government support through the approvals process — the clearest federal signal yet behind the AI build-out.

items.title: USSC: Powering the cloud — data centres and the future of Australia's grid
items.summary: Analysis of the collision between the AI build-out and the energy transition: where the grid can host GW-scale campuses, and where it can't.

items.title: DTA overhauls government AI policy: new AI Impact Assessment Tool and procurement guidance
items.summary: Updated Policy for the Responsible Use of AI in Government plus step-by-step AI procurement guidance (Plan–Source–Manage). Shapes every Commonwealth AI tender and PO from here on.

items.title: Victoria releases Sustainable Data Centre Action Plan as demand nearly doubles
items.summary: Victoria's data centre energy demand almost doubled in 12 months; the February 2026 action plan sets sustainability rules while Invest Victoria courts AI infrastructure.

items.title: Defence signs Deed of Standing Offer with Google Australia
items.summary: The Department of Defence solidified a standing-offer arrangement with Google Australia — one of a run of sovereign-cloud and AI deeds reshaping federal procurement in 2026.

items.title: OpenAI announces A$7 billion Australian investment
items.summary: Australia announced a A$7bn investment from OpenAI, part of the post-National-AI-Plan wave of global capital targeting Australian compute and AI capability.

items.title: Australia publishes its National AI Plan
items.source: Dept of Industry, Science and Resources
items.summary: Three goals, nine actions: build smart infrastructure, back domestic AI capability and attract global investment, and spread the benefits through adoption, skills and better public services. Government projects AI and automation could add up to A$600bn a year to GDP by 2030.

items.title: Amazon to invest A$20 billion in Australian data centre infrastructure (2025–2029)
items.summary: AWS pledges A$20bn through 2029 to expand Australian data centre capacity, backed by 11 renewable energy projects across NSW, Queensland and Victoria.

items.title: Blackstone completes ~A$24 billion acquisition of AirTrunk
items.summary: The largest data centre transaction in APAC history: Blackstone and CPP Investments acquire Sydney-headquartered AirTrunk, validating Australia as a global AI-infrastructure market.
